previous_findings_status.title: __SnapshotParams ignores [Sensitive]
previous_findings_status.evidence: Emission/Property/Data/this.cs:62-72 emits IsSensitive-aware mask for both PrValue and FinalValue. Discovery surfaces IsSensitive from [Sensitive] on the property symbol.

previous_findings_status.title: Variables.Resolve(input, skipInfrastructure=false) default
previous_findings_status.evidence: PLang/App/Variables/this.cs:393 unchanged. Convention finding, not regression.

previous_findings_status.title: Cycle/depth silent passthrough
previous_findings_status.evidence: Data/this.cs:575-578 returns FromError(VariableResolutionCycle, 400). Data/this.cs:583-587 returns FromError(ResolveDepthExceeded, 400).

previous_findings_status.title: FormatVerboseValue truncation
previous_findings_status.evidence: Errors/Error.cs:280-296 caps strings at 200 chars and JSON containers at 300 chars. Combined with v1 #1 fix, defense-in-depth is layered.

attack_surface.exposure: Data.AsT_Impl<T> at Data/this.cs:549-562 dispatches the literal slot string to T.Resolve(string, Context.@this) via reflection when typeof(IRawNameResolvable).IsAssignableFrom(typeof(T)). Bypasses %var% substitution branch entirely. Today only App.Variables.Variable implements the marker — used by 22 migrated action handlers (variable.{get,set,exists,clear,remove}, list.*, loop.foreach.{ItemName,KeyName}).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Slot string is from a signed .pr — trusted. T comes from compile-time generic instantiation in handler source — developer-controlled. ResolveMethodCache is keyed by Type and bounded by distinct generic instantiations.
mitigations: Public-static-only method lookup (BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Static) — cannot pull a private Resolve into dispatch
Exact signature match (string, Actor.Context.@this) — coincidental collisions improbable
Bypass condition gates on the marker interface — non-marker T (e.g. Data<string>) takes the existing %var% substitution path unchanged. No exposure widening for existing Ts.
Variable.Resolve is pure value-level transformation — no I/O, no cross-context reads, no recursion
gaps: Silent fallthrough when resolveMethod is null or resolvedObj is not T (codeanalyzer NIT-4, tester #2). See finding 2.
No try/catch around Invoke — a future T whose Resolve throws produces TargetInvocationException. App.Run catches it normally; not a vector but worth noting.

attack_surface.exposure: v7 deleted the RawScalarValidations block from Emission/Action/this.cs that previously emitted ServiceError for missing/null [VariableName] slots. Architect/v5 plan asserted [IsNotNull] would cover the case. Implementation applied [IsNotNull] in only 3/22 migrated handlers. The remaining 19 handlers crash with NRE on null-resolved Variable instead of returning a graceful ServiceError.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Slot value being null in .pr requires a malformed signed .pr — internal trust breach (developer/LLM/builder bug). User-sovereign threat model: not an external attack vector.
mitigations: Trust boundary holds — null/missing slot only reachable from signed-.pr content
FormatVerboseValue truncation still bounds disclosure if any error eventually surfaces
gaps: App.Run catch filter (PLang/App/this.cs:415) deliberately excludes NRE — null-Variable NRE is unrecoverable. Pre-v7 same case produced ServiceError. See finding 1.

attack_surface.exposure: App.Variables.Variable carries Name, RawValue, WasPercentWrapped via primary ctor + 1-arg helper. Implicit op string and override ToString both return v.Name. Used everywhere migrated handlers access the Variable's name.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Pure value type. RawValue is the verbatim slot string (signed .pr).
mitigations: Resolve is total — never throws, always returns a non-null Variable for any string input including null/empty (returns Variable with Name="")
gaps: Implicit op and ToString dereference v.Name — null Variable causes NRE. Reachable only when AsT_Impl returns a Data<Variable> with Value=null (raw was null or missing, bypass didn't fire). See finding 1.

Resolve accepts edge inputs that produce empty or special-character Names ("%%%" -> empty; "foo.bar" -> dot-path; "!app" -> infrastructure write). Same surface as legacy [VariableName] — not a v7 regression. See finding 3.

attack_surface.exposure: PLang/App/modules/variable/set.cs:103-108 CopyProperties iterates source.Properties and re-Sets each on target. Restores Properties survival across binding-mint that the runtime2-data-share-state refactor lost.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Same as pre-refactor variable.set — Properties carry per-Data result metadata from the source Data into the bound variable. Source is internal handler output.
mitigations: Variables.Set wraps non-Data values in fresh Data — subscribers don't propagate accidentally
gaps: If a Property carries a sensitive value (e.g. test snapshot Property containing a secret), it propagates verbatim. Same disclosure shape as the standing Variables.Snapshot() finding. No worsening — restoration of prior semantics.

findings.vector: After v7 deleted the RawScalarValidations safety net, 19 of 22 migrated handlers (variable.{get,set,exists,clear,remove}, list.{add,remove,reverse,set,sort,any/ListName,contains,count,first,flatten,get,group/ListName,indexof,join,last,unique}) carry no [IsNotNull] guard on their Data<Variable> Name slot. When the slot is missing from the .pr or has value=null, AsT_Impl<Variable>(null, ctx) falls through every resolution branch and returns Data<Variable> with Value=null and Success=true — no error captured. Generated property getter only sets __resolutionError when !Backing.Success. Handler executes, accesses Name.Value, implicit op (string)variable dereferences null Variable -> NullReferenceException. App.Run's catch filter at PLang/App/this.cs:415 deliberately excludes NRE, so the exception propagates uncaught and crashes action dispatch. Pre-v7 the same case produced a graceful ServiceError via the now-deleted RawScalarValidations block.
findings.preconditions: Signed .pr with a Data<Variable> slot whose value is null or missing entirely. Reachable from malformed builder output, hand-edited .pr, or LLM emission bugs in the builder pipeline.
findings.impact: Unrecoverable hard crash of action dispatch. App.Run's catch deliberately excludes NRE because NRE typically indicates a real bug. Trust boundary holds (.pr is signed) so this is not an external attack — it's a robustness regression: graceful ServiceError replaced with hard NRE crash.
findings.exploit_sketch: Author writes a .pr step `variable.set Name(null)` (or missing Name slot) and signs it. Plang dispatches variable.set. __ResolveData('Name') -> NotFound. NotFound.As<Variable>(ctx) -> AsT_Impl bypass skipped (raw is not string), all branches fall through to WrapAs(null, ctx) -> ConstructWrap<Variable>(default, ctx) -> Data<Variable>{Value=null, Success=true}. Generated getter doesn't set __resolutionError. Run() does Variables.Get(Name.Value) -> implicit op dereferences null Variable -> NRE. App.Run filter excludes NRE -> uncaught crash. Same shape for the other 18 handlers that read or write through Name.Value / ListName.Value.
findings.proposed_fix: Best fix at the generator: in Emission/Property/Data/this.cs:EmitProperty, when InnerType is a marker IRawNameResolvable (or for any non-nullable Data<T> after As<T> resolution), emit a not-null check on Backing.Value. If Backing.Value is null and !IsNullable, set __resolutionError = Data.@this.FromError(new ServiceError("Required parameter '{ParamName}' is missing or null", "MissingRequiredParameter", 400)). One-shot fix at the wrapper level enforces the architect's intended 'Variable.Resolve produces non-null Variable, Data<Variable>.Value is never null' contract instead of relying on per-handler [IsNotNull]. Alternative: make Variable's op_Implicit and ToString null-safe (return string.Empty when v is null) — simpler but masks the missing-parameter case as empty-string, hiding the .pr error from diagnostics.

findings.vector: Data/this.cs:549-562 IRawNameResolvable bypass dispatches via reflection. If T : IRawNameResolvable but T does not declare a public static Resolve(string, Actor.Context.@this), the cache returns null. The bypass falls through silently to the %var% substitution branch — exactly the bare-name regression v7 was designed to prevent. Same fallthrough occurs if Resolve returns an object that isn't of type T. Today only Variable implements the marker so the trap is theoretical, but a future implementer who forgets Resolve (or misnames it, or returns a derived type that fails the `is T` check) silently re-introduces the regression.
findings.preconditions: A new type implementing IRawNameResolvable without a properly-shaped Resolve method, used as Data<T> on an action handler property.
findings.impact: Silent reversion to the bare-name regression: slot Data<Variable>'s slot key (e.g. literal 'Name') reads as the variable name instead of the developer's intent. Could miswrite to an unintended variable in the user's namespace.
findings.exploit_sketch: Future maintainer adds `public sealed record Tag(string Value) : IRawNameResolvable;` without a Resolve method. Handler declares `partial Data<Tag> X`. Slot value `%foo%` enters AsT_Impl<Tag>. Bypass detects marker, ResolveMethodCache.GetOrAdd returns null (no matching method), fall-through. %var% substitution then runs: Variables.Get('foo') -> not found -> notFound Data<Tag> with Value=null. Handler crashes or silently does the wrong thing. This is exactly what the marker was meant to prevent.
findings.proposed_fix: In the bypass branch, when typeof(T) implements IRawNameResolvable but resolveMethod is null, return Data<T>.FromError(new ServiceError($"Type {typeof(T).Name} implements IRawNameResolvable but lacks public static Resolve(string, Actor.Context.@this)", "MissingResolveMethod", 500)). Same arm when resolvedObj is not T. Fail-fast at the contract boundary instead of silent fallthrough.

findings.vector: Variable.Resolve accepts any string and produces a Variable. Edge inputs: '%%%' produces Variable{Name=''} (empty Name with WasPercentWrapped=true). '%a%b%' produces Variable{Name='a%b'} (mid-string % preserved because Trim only strips edges). 'foo.bar' produces Variable{Name='foo.bar'} which Variables.Set then parses as a dot-path. '!app' produces Variable{Name='!app'} which Variables.Set will overwrite as an infrastructure variable. The empty-Name path causes Variables.Set('', value) to store under empty key while Variables.Get('') returns NotFound — silent data-loss footgun. The infrastructure-variable case is identical to the legacy [VariableName] surface.
findings.preconditions: Handler-author or LLM-generated .pr emits a slot whose stripped Name is empty, contains mid-string %, contains dot/bracket separators, or starts with !.
findings.impact: Silent data loss (empty Name), unexpected dot-path navigation (foo.bar), or infrastructure-variable overwrite (!app). All within signed-.pr trust boundary — same surface as legacy [VariableName]; not a v7 regression.
findings.exploit_sketch: Variable.Resolve('!app', ctx) -> Variable{Name='!app'}. Handler does Variables.Set('!app', maliciousValue). Subsequent reads of %!app% return the malicious value. Existing PLang behavior since [VariableName] — not new. Listed for completeness.
findings.proposed_fix: Optional defensive validation: reject empty Name (return Variable with WasPercentWrapped + an explicit IsValid=false flag, or have AsT_Impl surface FromError when Variable.Name is empty). Aligns Variable's CleanName logic with Variables.CleanName so the two views of the same string don't disagree.

findings.vector: Variables.Resolve(input, skipInfrastructure: false) is the default. Untrusted external input (HTTP responses, file content, LLM output) processed through Resolve without skipInfrastructure=true will expand %!var% references — leaking infrastructure variables into externally-visible output. Carried forward from v1 finding #2; unchanged.
findings.preconditions: A callsite that runs Variables.Resolve on attacker-influenced string without the skipInfrastructure flag.
findings.impact: Disclosure of host paths, app identifiers, or other %!var% values. Severity remains low because PLang is user-sovereign — real risk only if a handler does Resolve on transport-channel content without the flag.
findings.exploit_sketch: Same as v1 #2 — handler reads HTTP response into %resp%, then logs Variables.Resolve('Got: %resp%'). If %resp% body contains '%!app.AbsolutePath%', the Resolve walk substitutes it.
findings.proposed_fix: Audit callsites of Variables.Resolve to confirm skipInfrastructure=true at every untrusted boundary. Consider inverting the default — safer-by-default, low blast radius for internal callers.

summary: Re-audit on the v7 Variable + IRawNameResolvable migration. Two of v1's four findings closed: __SnapshotParams now respects [Sensitive] (v1 #1) and the cycle/depth path now FromErrors instead of silent passthrough (v1 #3). The new bypass branch in Data.AsT_Impl narrows the resolution surface for marker types in a defensible way — public-static-only method lookup, exact signature match, bounded reflection cache, no exposure widening for non-marker Ts. Two robustness gaps emerged from the architect-vs-implementation drift: the RawScalarValidations safety net was deleted under the assumption that [IsNotNull] would cover the missing-parameter case, but only 3 of 22 migrated handlers carry [IsNotNull], so null/missing Name slots now NRE-crash where v6 returned graceful ServiceError. The IRawNameResolvable bypass falls through silently when a future T implements the marker without Resolve — codeanalyzer NIT-4 + tester #2 already raised this. Both are low-severity under the user-sovereign threat model (.pr is signed; null/missing slots reachable only from malformed signed content) but worth a defense-in-depth fix at the generator level. Variable.Resolve accepts edge inputs (empty/special-character Names) — same surface as legacy [VariableName], not a regression. v1 #2 (skipInfrastructure default) carries forward unchanged. No critical/high open. Verdict: PASS.
